info.title: MICROSERVICE_DEV_Subscriber Management API
info.description: The Subscriber Onboarding API facilitates the process of adding new subscribers to the system. This endpoint is typically used by businesses seeking to expand their customer base or enhance user engagement. It allows for the collection of necessary subscriber information and enables seamless integration into marketing or communication strategies. Common consumers of this API include marketing teams and customer relationship management systems looking to streamline subscription processes.

post.summary: Create a new subscriber for the customer.
post.description: This endpoint allows you to create a new subscriber linked to a specific customer. By providing the necessary subscriber details, you can effectively manage subscriptions and enhance customer engagement.

requestBody.description: Request body to create a new subscriber for customers. This should include all necessary information, formatted as JSON, to successfully register a subscriber. Ensure that the data adheres to the specified schema.

404.description: The requested resource was not found.

500.description: An unexpected error occurred on the server.

200.description: The request was successfully processed and the response contains the expected data.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createCustomerSubscribe.title: 404 Error Response for POST Operations on Subscribers

timestamp.description: The timestamp when the error occurred.

status.description: The status of the error response indicating success (false) or failure (true).

error.description: A brief description of the error occurred.

message.description: A detailed message explaining the error.
message.example: Customer details not found for subscriber activation.

path.description: The API path where the error occurred.

trace.description: A trace of the error occurrence for debugging purposes.

details.description: Detailed information about the error including customer-related data.

suggestion.description: A suggestion on how to resolve the encountered issue.
suggestion.example: Verify customer information and retry the request.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createCustomerSubscribe.description: Schema representing the 404 error response for POST operations involving subscribers. It includes details about the error, timestamps, and potential suggestions.

example.message: Customer details not found for subscriber activation.

details.message: No registered customer profile exists for the provided customer ID.

example.suggestion: Verify customer information and retry the request.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createCustomerSubscribe.title: ErrorDetails

service.description: The name of the service that encountered the error.

errorCode.description: A specific code identifying the type of error.

message.description: A detailed message regarding the error.

database.description: The database that was being accessed when the error occurred.

retryable.description: Indicates whether the request can be retried.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createCustomerSubscribe.description: This schema provides additional details about the error that occurred.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createCustomerSubscribe.title: ErrorResponse

timestamp.description: The timestamp when the error occurred.

status.description: Indicates whether the request was successful or not.

message.description: A detailed message regarding the error.
message.example: Unable to process subscriber activation request due to internal service failure.

path.description: The API path that was called when the error occurred.

trace.description: A stack trace or additional information about the error.

suggestion.description: A suggestion for the user on what to do next.
suggestion.example: Please retry after some time or contact support if the issue persists.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createCustomerSubscribe.description: This schema represents the response structure for a 500 Internal Server Error when processing a POST request.

example.message: Unable to process subscriber activation request due to internal service failure.

details.message: Database connection timeout while saving subscriber details.

example.suggestion: Please retry after some time or contact support if the issue persists.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createCustomerSubscribe.title: Subscriber Creation Request

customerId.description: The unique identifier for the customer.

msisdn.description: Mobile Station International Subscriber Directory Number (MSISDN) associated with the subscriber.

plan.description: The subscription plan chosen by the subscriber.

network.description: The network provider associated with the subscriber.

activationDate.description: Date when the subscription was activated, in ISO 8601 format.

deviceType.description: Type of device used by the subscriber.

simType.description: Type of SIM card issued to the subscriber.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createCustomerSubscribe.description: Schema for creating a new subscriber, including personal and account-related information.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createCustomerSubscribe.title: Subscriber Response

customerId.description: Unique identifier for the customer associated with the subscriber.

msisdn.description: Mobile Station International Subscriber Directory Number (MSISDN) associated with the subscriber.

plan.description: The subscription plan that the subscriber is enrolled in.

network.description: The network provider for the subscriber.

deviceType.description: The type of device used by the subscriber.

simType.description: The type of SIM card associated with the subscriber.

activatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the subscriber was activated.

message.description: A message related to the subscriber's operation.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createCustomerSubscribe.description: Represents the response details for a subscriber operation.

environment.name: environment
environment.description: Environment used to select the workflow branch.

serviceCategory.name: serviceCategory
serviceCategory.description: Service category used to select the workflow branch.

Vary.description: Indicates how the response may vary based on different criteria, typically used for caching purposes.

Location.description: Specifies the URI of the newly created resource resulting from the POST operation.

content-type.description: Indicates the media type of the resource being returned, specifying how the content should be interpreted.
